name: Immix — Mark-Region with Opportunistic Evacuation
summary: Blackburn and McKinley's Immix (PLDI 2008) is a mark-region collector that allocates and reclaims memory in contiguous regions at coarse block grain when possible and finer line grain otherwise — defining a fourth GC family alongside semi-space, mark-sweep, and mark-compact.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Blackburn and McKinley's **Immix** (PLDI 2008) is a mark-region collector that allocates and reclaims memory in contiguous regions at a coarse block grain when possible and finer line grain otherwise — defining a fourth GC family alongside semi-space, mark-sweep, and mark-compact. The core insight is that bump allocation, reuse, and selective compaction can coexist in one heap structure if the heap is partitioned at two levels (blocks and lines).

Immix is foundational: the basis for Rust's experimental GC, Scala Native, MMTk's flagship plan, Ruby/Julia MMTk bindings, and LXR.

sections.title: Block/line hierarchy and opportunistic evacuation
sections.content:
Two-level block (32 KB) / line (128 B) hierarchy enables bump-allocation into partially-free blocks. **Opportunistic evacuation** mixes copying and marking in a single pass — the collector evacuates only fragmented source blocks when target space is available, otherwise marks in place. The same allocator path serves mutator and collector, achieving 7–25% improvement over canonical collectors.

The block-and-line structure is what makes Immix attractive as a substrate for other collectors: request-private GC (Iso) uses 32 KB blocks as request-scoped reclamation units; LXR layers reference counting on top of the same hierarchy.

sections.title: Deployment and downstream consumers
sections.content:
Immix is the substrate for several production and research runtimes:

- **MMTk** ships Immix, GenImmix, and StickyImmix as flagship plans.
- **Ruby 3.4 Modular GC** (January 2025) ships an MMTk-based Immix experimental plan.
- **Scala Native** uses Immix as its default collector.
- **LXR** (PLDI 2022) layers reference counting on top of Immix.
- **Iso** (PLDI 2025) reuses Immix's 32 KB block structure for request-private reclamation.

Status (as of 2026-04): production-ready as a GC algorithm; deployed via MMTk and language-specific ports.
